title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Binance posts over $400M in weekly net outflows as MiCA deadline nears
summary: Early exchange flow data show no clear signs of a mass migration from Binance ahead of the EU MiCA transition deadline, despite rivals' efforts to attract users.

title: By The Way: Interesting Headlines You May Have Missed
summary: Hacky sack makes a comeback, the US Men's National soccer team plays with some puppies, Doctors vs Podcast advise, and Robotaxi rules easing. Lisa Mateo, Christina Ruffini and David Gura dive into the headlines you may have missed on Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aramco Helicopter Crash in Ras Tanura Kills All 14 on Board
summary: A helicopter operated by Saudi Aramco crashed Sunday in the Saudi city of Ras Tanura, killing all 14 passengers on board, the state-owned SPA news agency reported.

title: Galaxy Research Cuts CLARITY Act Passage Odds to 50-50 as Senate Clock Runs Out
summary:

Galaxy Research Cuts CLARITY Act Passage Odds to 50-50 as Senate Clock Runs Out
Galaxy Research cut its odds of the Senate passing the CLARITY Act to 50-50, citing dwindling time before Congress's August recess and mounting legislative uncertainty.

title: The Future Cyberpunk Imagined Is Here: How Much Did It Get Right?
summary: From AI to brain implants, cyberpunk got more right than many people realize. Its biggest prediction, however, wasn't the technology.

title: The Stablecoin Founder Map Doesn't Match the Stablecoin Volume Map
summary: Emerging markets drive most real-world stablecoin usage, yet founder concentration and venture funding remain U.S.- and Europe-centric.

title: SecondFi maps recovery path after $2.4 million Cardano wallet exploit, aims to return funds within two weeks
summary: An exploit drained about $2.4 million in ADA from 374 addresses over three days through a flaw in SecondFi's wallet-generation software.

title: Average IBIT investor now down about 40% as spot bitcoin ETFs cap second-worst week on record
